---
title: "Protein LMs + GNNs — Lesson Suite"
toc: false
---

A hands-on teaching suite on **protein language models (pLM)**, **graph neural
networks (GNN)** for proteins, and **model explainability**. Every page is a
Jupyter notebook rendered with its saved outputs — plots, tables, and metrics
included — so you can read the completed lessons without running anything.

Use the sidebar (or the search box) to browse. The tracks build up roughly in
order:

## Tracks

- **Protein Language Models (pLM)** — embeddings and probes, zero-shot variant
  effect, fine-tuning and token classification, model comparison, attention
  contacts, LoRA/PEFT, structure-aware models, retrieval, inverse folding, and
  calibration.
- **Graph Neural Networks (GNN)** — building graphs from proteins, node and
  graph classification, fusing pLM features with GNNs, equivariance, real
  structures, edge features, contact prediction, kNN graphs, oversmoothing, and
  interaction graphs.
- **Explainability — Idea-4** — an interaction-explainability research track on
  PINDER / SKEMPI: attention, integrated gradients, GNNExplainer, and their
  consensus for hotspot recovery.
- **Capstones** — an end-to-end pLM→GNN pipeline and a multi-model benchmark
  suite.

## How this site is built

The notebooks are executed locally (on GPU, via the
[`experiments/`](https://github.com/LampOfSocrates/xai-starter/tree/main/experiments)
papermill runner) and committed **with their outputs**. This site is published
by [Quarto](https://quarto.org) with re-execution disabled, so it always shows
exactly the outputs in the repository — no models or GPU are needed to view it.

> Two notebooks (`gnn_l2`, `plm_demo`) currently have no saved outputs; run them
> once locally and re-commit to populate their pages.
